# UC-19.1.44 · OpenStack Nova Compute Instance Build and Spawn Failure Rate

## Description

Nova is where tenant VM requests become running guests. Repeated spawn failures usually indicate scheduler filters, image problems, exhausted compute-node CPU or RAM, or Neutron port binding failures. Tracking failure strings by compute node isolates the blast radius.

## Value

Protects tenant-facing IaaS SLAs by catching Nova build failure clusters before help-desk volume spikes.

## Implementation

Collect `nova-compute` and `nova-conductor` logs via UF. If using JSON oslo, use `spath` or INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS for `message`. Normalize `instance_uuid`. Alert on rolling-window failure counts per compute node. Correlate with placement inventory and Cinder volume attach errors.

## SPL

```spl
index=openstack sourcetype="openstack:nova" earliest=-1h
| search ("Failed to spawn" OR "NoValidHost" OR "Instance failed" OR "BuildAbortException")
| stats count as failures by host, instance_uuid
| where failures>=3
| sort -failures
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (failures by compute node), Table (instance_uuid samples), Timeline (error class).

## References

- [OpenStack Nova — Troubleshooting](https://docs.openstack.org/nova/latest/admin/troubleshooting.html)
